title: erophila verna var. stelligera
intro: Erophila verna var. stelligera, also known as spring whitlow-grass, is a perennial flowering plant in the mustard family, characterized by its small, white flowers and hairy stems. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, making it useful for treating respiratory issues and skin irritations. The health benefits of spring whitlow-grass include alleviating symptoms of bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as soothing skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of spring whitlow-grass can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
